{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet Manager",
  "description": "The vision of Samcham Holdings Logistics Limited is to become a leading logistics and transportation solutions provider recognized for operational reliability, safety excellence, and superior service delivery within Nigeria and across regional markets, to provide efficient, dependable, and safe logistics services through disciplined operational management, a competent workforce, and the adoption of industry best practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a proactive and field-oriented Fleet Supervisor to oversee daily fleet and driver operations across multiple business locations.
	The ideal candidate must demonstrate strong on-ground presence, operational awareness, and the ability to manage drivers, vehicles, and logistics activities in real time.
	This role requires a hands-on professional who thrives in field operations and can ensure compliance, efficiency, safety, and accountability across all fleet activities.


Key Responsibilities
Fleet Operations Supervision:


	Provide active on-field supervision of fleet operations across company locations and loading points.
	Monitor truck dispatch, movement, loading, and offloading activities to ensure operational efficiency.
	Ensure that trucks are deployed according to operational schedules and delivery timelines.


Driver Management &amp; Discipline:


	Supervise and manage drivers to ensure compliance with company policies and operational procedures.
	Monitor driver behavior, attendance, punctuality, and adherence to safety standards.
	Address driver-related issues and escalate disciplinary matters where necessary.


Fleet Monitoring &amp; Performance:


	Track fleet utilization, turnaround time, and operational performance.
	Identify operational delays, inefficiencies, or irregularities and take corrective action.
	Ensure vehicles operate within approved routes and schedules.


Compliance &amp; Documentation:
Verify and ensure proper documentation for all trips including:


	Waybills
	Trip sheets
	Delivery confirmations
	Vehicle inspection reports
	Ensure drivers carry valid licenses, permits, and required operational documents.


Safety &amp; Operational Standards:


	Enforce strict adherence to safety regulations and company policies.
	Conduct regular vehicle and driver compliance checks.
	Ensure drivers comply with road safety rules and operational safety guidelines.


Incident Management:
Respond promptly to operational incidents such as:


	Vehicle breakdowns
	Accidents
	Delivery disruptions
	Coordinate recovery efforts and report incidents to management in real time.


Reporting &amp; Communication:


	Provide daily operational updates and reports on fleet performance.
	Maintain accurate records of truck movements, driver assignments, and operational activities.
	Communicate operational issues promptly to management.


Stakeholder Coordination:


	Work closely with warehouse teams, loading supervisors, and operational staff.
	Ensure smooth coordination between dispatch locations and delivery destinations.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Minimum of OND / HND / B.Sc. in Logistics, Transport Management, or related field.
	Minimum 4 &ndash; 7 years of experience in fleet supervision or logistics operations.
	Strong understanding of trucking operations and driver management.


Ideal Candidate Profile:

The ideal candidate should:


	Prefer working in the field rather than the office.
	Be highly responsive to operational issues.
	Demonstrate strong ownership of fleet performance and driver discipline.
	Be able to manage multiple operational locations effectively.


Required Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong leadership and supervision skills
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making ability
	Strong field coordination capability
	Ability to work under pressure and respond to operational challenges
	Good communication and reporting skills
	High level of discipline, accountability, and integrity.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV,&nbsp;highlighting relevant logistics or fleet management experience to:&nbsp;humanresources@ilsfleetmgt.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-03-26",

Fleet Manager Job at Samcham Holdings Logistics Limited




        
    

        
        The vision of Samcham Holdings Logistics Limited is to become a leading logistics and transportation solutions provider recognized for operational reliability, safety excellence, and superior service delivery within Nigeria and across regional markets, to provide efficient, dependable, and safe logistics services through disciplined operational management, a competent workforce, and the adoption of industry best practices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Manager
Job Location: Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and field-oriented Fleet Supervisor to oversee daily fleet and driver operations across multiple business locations.
	The ideal candidate must demonstrate strong on-ground presence, operational awareness, and the ability to manage drivers, vehicles, and logistics activities in real time.
	This role requires a hands-on professional who thrives in field operations and can ensure compliance, efficiency, safety, and accountability across all fleet activities.


Key Responsibilities
Fleet Operations Supervision:


	Provide active on-field supervision of fleet operations across company locations and loading points.
	Monitor truck dispatch, movement, loading, and offloading activities to ensure operational efficiency.
	Ensure that trucks are deployed according to operational schedules and delivery timelines.


Driver Management & Discipline:


	Supervise and manage drivers to ensure compliance with company policies and operational procedures.
	Monitor driver behavior, attendance, punctuality, and adherence to safety standards.
	Address driver-related issues and escalate disciplinary matters where necessary.


Fleet Monitoring & Performance:


	Track fleet utilization, turnaround time, and operational performance.
	Identify operational delays, inefficiencies, or irregularities and take corrective action.
	Ensure vehicles operate within approved routes and schedules.


Compliance & Documentation:
Verify and ensure proper documentation for all trips including:


	Waybills
	Trip sheets
	Delivery confirmations
	Vehicle inspection reports
	Ensure drivers carry valid licenses, permits, and required operational documents.


Safety & Operational Standards:


	Enforce strict adherence to safety regulations and company policies.
	Conduct regular vehicle and driver compliance checks.
	Ensure drivers comply with road safety rules and operational safety guidelines.


Incident Management:
Respond promptly to operational incidents such as:


	Vehicle breakdowns
	Accidents
	Delivery disruptions
	Coordinate recovery efforts and report incidents to management in real time.


Reporting & Communication:


	Provide daily operational updates and reports on fleet performance.
	Maintain accurate records of truck movements, driver assignments, and operational activities.
	Communicate operational issues promptly to management.


Stakeholder Coordination:


	Work closely with warehouse teams, loading supervisors, and operational staff.
	Ensure smooth coordination between dispatch locations and delivery destinations.


Qualifications & Experience


	Minimum of OND / HND / B.Sc. in Logistics, Transport Management, or related field.
	Minimum 4 – 7 years of experience in fleet supervision or logistics operations.
	Strong understanding of trucking operations and driver management.


Ideal Candidate Profile:

The ideal candidate should:


	Prefer working in the field rather than the office.
	Be highly responsive to operational issues.
	Demonstrate strong ownership of fleet performance and driver discipline.
	Be able to manage multiple operational locations effectively.


Required Skills & Competencies:


	Strong leadership and supervision skills
	Excellent problem-solving and decision-making ability
	Strong field coordination capability
	Ability to work under pressure and respond to operational challenges
	Good communication and reporting skills
	High level of discipline, accountability, and integrity.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV, highlighting relevant logistics or fleet management experience to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Carbon Nigeria
content:
Carbon is a pan-African digital bank with a mission to provide friction-free finance to its customers. Carbon promises to play a fundamental role in its customers’ lives wherever they are, with flexible solutions. We pride ourselves on our efficiency and with just $10mm of equity raised in 2015, we have disbursed over $100m in loans and earned more than $30mm in revenue over the last 2 years. Carbon has operations in Ghana, Kenya, and Nigeria, supported by a talented team spread between Lagos, Nairobi, London, Argentina, and Palo Alto so we operate with a remote-first mindset. 

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Collection Executive

Job Location: Lagos
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Description


	The Telephone Collection Executive is responsible for contacting customers with overdue loan obligations to secure repayment and resolve delinquent accounts.
	The role requires strong negotiation, communication, and customer engagement skills to achieve recovery targets while maintaining professionalism and compliance with company policies.
	The agent will engage customers via phone calls, emails, and SMS to obtain payment commitments, follow up on promises to pay, and escalate difficult cases when necessary.


Key Responsibilities 


	Contact customers with overdue accounts via phone to discuss repayment of outstanding balances.
	Negotiate and secure promises to pay (PTPs) and ensure timely follow-up on commitments.
	Educate customers on their loan obligations, repayment options, and consequences of default.
	Maintain accurate records of all customer interactions in the collections system.
	Send follow-up SMS or emails to reinforce repayment commitments.
	Escalate difficult, disputed, or unresponsive accounts to supervisors when required.
	Meet assigned daily call targets and monthly recovery goals.
	Maintain professionalism and comply with ethical collections practices and regulatory guidelines.


Requirements


	Strong communication, persuasion, and negotiation skills.
	Ability to manage difficult conversations with delinquent customers professionally.
	Comfortable working in a target-driven environment.
	Basic knowledge of Microsoft Excel or CRM/collections systems is an advantage.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.


Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) Field Recovery Agent 

Job Location: Lagos

Job Description


	The Field Recovery Agent is responsible for engaging delinquent customers in person to facilitate loan repayment, resolve payment challenges, and recover outstanding debts.
	The role requires strong communication, negotiation skills, and the ability to represent the company professionally while ensuring compliance with recovery policies and regulatory guidelines.


Key Responsibilities


	Visit delinquent customers at their residential or business locations to follow up on overdue payments.
	Negotiate and secure repayment commitments (PTPs) and ensure prompt follow-up.
	Educate customers on their outstanding obligations and repayment options.
	Verify customer information including address, business location, and employment status where necessary.
	Document visit outcomes and update the collections system with accurate field reports.
	Escalate difficult cases or disputes to the internal collections team promptly.
	Maintain professional conduct and adhere strictly to company recovery policies and regulatory standards.


Requirements


	Previous experience in field collections, loan recovery, sales, or customer engagement is an advantage.
	Strong negotiation and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to handle difficult conversations professionally.
	Must be familiar with the assigned location/territory.
	Ability to work independently and meet recovery targets.
	Access to a smartphone for reporting and communication.
	High level of integrity, professionalism, and confidentiality.  


Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) SME Onboarding Specialist

Job Location: Lagos
Job type: Full-time

Responsibilities
You will be responsible for


	This role is focused on the external legalization and formalization of business entities.
	Navigating regulatory portals to ensure our SME leads are properly registered and ready for banking activation within our internal SLAs.
	Pre-Onboarding Audit: Review customer-provided data (IDs, addresses, and name options) to ensure 100% compliance before external submission.
	Stakeholder Coordination: While internal KYC is handled by our KYC/Compliance team, you will ensure the external legal entity is created and ready for handoff to the activation desk.


Requirements
You must have:


	A minimum of 1 year of hands-on experience using the CAC Portal.
	Working knowledge of the automated stamp duty process and documentation requirements for Business Names and LTDs.
	Proven ability to handle high-volume administrative tasks with a near-zero error rate.
	Ability to clearly explain regulatory requirements to small business owners.


Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "The No Bored Community is an organization relentlessly dedicated to transforming mental health wellness across Nigeria and African societies. We envision a future where mental health is openly discussed, properly supported, and fundamentally valued as a cornerstone of individual well-being and national prosperity.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Content Writer

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria

Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications


Why Join?


	Build real-world experience
	Get a certificate &amp; LOR
	Receive mentorship
	Network with industry professionals
	Contribute just 1 hour/week remotely


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;omorakalucy2@gmail.com&nbsp;or&nbsp;meritprecious818@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of th email.

Note:&nbsp;For more information contact the recruiter on&nbsp;+234 807 151 1773 or +234 703 084 8649.",

Content Writer Job at the No Bored Community




        
    

        
        The No Bored Community is an organization relentlessly dedicated to transforming mental health wellness across Nigeria and African societies. We envision a future where mental health is openly discussed, properly supported, and fundamentally valued as a cornerstone of individual well-being and national prosperity.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Content Writer

Job Location: Nigeria

Qualifications
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications


Why Join?


	Build real-world experience
	Get a certificate & LOR
	Receive mentorship
	Network with industry professionals
	Contribute just 1 hour/week remotely


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] or [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of th email.

Note: For more information contact the recruiter on +234 807 151 1773 or +234 703 084 8649.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE Officer",
  "description": "Rotic Aluminium Extrusion -&nbsp;We are a steadily growing aluminium extrusion company in Anambra. We are dedicated to delivering the highest standard of aluminium extrusion profiles. Our seasoned team of experts ensures that each product we create embodies precision and craftsmanship.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Awka, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are currently seeking a Safety Officer at our facility in Awka, Anambra State, to effectively manage and enforce safety protocols, ensuring a safe working environment on the company&#39;s shop floor.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and oversee safety protocols and procedures on the shop floor.
	Conduct regular safety inspections and audits to ensure compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Identify potential hazards and take proactive measures to prevent accidents and injuries.
	Investigate any accidents or incidents on the shop floor and prepare detailed reports.
	Conduct safety training sessions for employees to promote a culture of safety.
	Ensure that all safety equipment and devices are properly maintained and in good working condition.
	Maintain up-to-date records of safety inspections, incidents, and training activities.
	Prepare and put up safety signages regularly.
	Stay informed about the latest safety regulations and best practices in the industry.
	Prepare and submit regular reports on safety activities and performance to management.


Skills and Qualifications


	Minimum of HND in Occupational Health and Safety, Industrial Hygiene, or a related field.
	2 - 5 years of proven experience as a Safety Officer in a manufacturing or industrial setting.
	Strong knowledge of safety regulations, standards, and best practices.
	Excellent attention to detail and observational skills.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to conduct thorough investigations and prepare detailed reports.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite especially Microsoft Excel.
	Strong problem-solving skills and ability to implement practical solutions.
	Ability to work well under pressure and manage multiple tasks.
	Certification in occupational health and safety (e.g., NEBOSH, OSHA) is preferred.


Benefits We Offer


	Salary: N300,000 monthly.
	Comfortable accommodation.
	A free meal daily.
	Solid bonus system.
	Medical bills coverage.


Note: Additional Requirement for Onsite Living: Candidates willing to live onsite in factory accommodation in Awka are strongly encouraged to apply.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-02",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "name": "Rotic Aluminium"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",

      "addressCountry": "NG"

HSE Officer Job at Rotic Aluminium Extrusion




        
    

        
        Rotic Aluminium Extrusion - We are a steadily growing aluminium extrusion company in Anambra. We are dedicated to delivering the highest standard of aluminium extrusion profiles. Our seasoned team of experts ensures that each product we create embodies precision and craftsmanship.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer

Job Location: Awka, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are currently seeking a Safety Officer at our facility in Awka, Anambra State, to effectively manage and enforce safety protocols, ensuring a safe working environment on the company's shop floor.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and oversee safety protocols and procedures on the shop floor.
	Conduct regular safety inspections and audits to ensure compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.
	Identify potential hazards and take proactive measures to prevent accidents and injuries.
	Investigate any accidents or incidents on the shop floor and prepare detailed reports.
	Conduct safety training sessions for employees to promote a culture of safety.
	Ensure that all safety equipment and devices are properly maintained and in good working condition.
	Maintain up-to-date records of safety inspections, incidents, and training activities.
	Prepare and put up safety signages regularly.
	Stay informed about the latest safety regulations and best practices in the industry.
	Prepare and submit regular reports on safety activities and performance to management.


Skills and Qualifications


	Minimum of HND in Occupational Health and Safety, Industrial Hygiene, or a related field.
	2 - 5 years of proven experience as a Safety Officer in a manufacturing or industrial setting.
	Strong knowledge of safety regulations, standards, and best practices.
	Excellent attention to detail and observational skills.
	Effective communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to conduct thorough investigations and prepare detailed reports.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite especially Microsoft Excel.
	Strong problem-solving skills and ability to implement practical solutions.
	Ability to work well under pressure and manage multiple tasks.
	Certification in occupational health and safety (e.g., NEBOSH, OSHA) is preferred.


Benefits We Offer


	Salary: N300,000 monthly.
	Comfortable accommodation.
	A free meal daily.
	Solid bonus system.
	Medical bills coverage.


Note: Additional Requirement for Onsite Living: Candidates willing to live onsite in factory accommodation in Awka are strongly encouraged to apply.    

    

        Click here to Apply
